content: read reviews/53.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/53.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/53.md.

1→# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist — `deepmind/53.lean`
     2→
     3→Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
     4→without an explicit justification.
     5→
     6→---
     7→
     8→## 1. Copyright Header
     9→
    10→- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
    11→  - Lines 1–15 match the required block verbatim.
    12→- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.
    13→
    14→## 2. Import Statement
    15→
    16→- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line).
    17→  - Line 17: `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
    18→- [x] No other imports are present.
    19→
    20→## 3. Module Docstring
    21→
    22→- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
    23→  - Lines 19–32.
    24→- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 53`.
    25→- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source.
    26→  - `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/53](https://www.erdosproblems.com/53)`
    27→- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
    28→  - Describes the sum-or-product-of-distinct-elements condition and notes the problem was solved by Chang.
    29→- [x] Optional: bibliography entries for related papers.
    30→  - Two entries: `[ErSz83]` (Erdős–Szemerédi) and `[Ch03]` (Chang).
    31→
    32→## 4. Namespace
    33→
    34→- [x] All declarations are inside a namespace (`namespace Erdos53 ... end Erdos53`).
    35→  - Lines 36 and 67.
    36→- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos53`).
    37→- [x] `open` statements appear **before** the namespace block.
    38→  - Line 34: `open Finset BigOperators` precedes `namespace Erdos53` on line 36.
    39→
    40→## 5. Custom Definitions
    41→
    42→- [x] Helper `def` declarations have a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    43→  - `subsetSums` (lines 38–43) and `subsetProducts` (lines 45–50) both have docstrings.
    44→- [x] Definitions that involve classical choice or nonconstructive operations are marked `noncomputable`.
    45→  - N/A — both definitions are computable (`Finset.powerset.image` over decidable operations).
    46→- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
    47→- [x] Types and Props use `UpperCamelCase`.
    48→  - N/A — no custom types or Props defined.
    49→- [x] Functions returning types use `lowerCamelCase`.
    50→  - `subsetSums` and `subsetProducts` are correctly `lowerCamelCase`.
    51→- [x] Use `local notation` for problem-specific notation, scoped inside the namespace.
    52→  - N/A — no custom notation used.
    53→- [ ] Consider adding `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for new definitions.
    54→  - Not present. Optional but would improve confidence that `subsetSums` and `subsetProducts` behave as expected (e.g., a test that `subsetSums {1,2} = {0,1,2,3}`).
    55→
    56→## 6. Theorem Statements
    57→
    58→- [x] Benchmark problems use the `theorem` keyword.
    59→  - Line 61: `theorem erdos_53`.
    60→- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_53` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
    61→- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions: `snake_case` for terms of Props.
    62→- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` attributes on the **same attribute line**.
    63→  - Line 60: `@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]`.
    64→- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    65→  - Lines 52–58.
    66→- [x] Theorem docstring references source papers.
    67→  - References `[ErSz83]` and `[Ch03]`.
    68→
    69→### `answer()` usage
    70→
    71→The original problem asks: *"Is it true that, for every k, if |A| is sufficiently large depending on k, then there are at least |A|^k many integers which are either the sum or product of distinct elements of A?"*
    72→
    73→This is a **yes/no question** ("Is it true that..."), so **Case 1** applies.
    74→
    75→- [x] The theorem type is `answer(True) ↔ P`.
    76→  - The problem is solved affirmatively by Chang [Ch03], so `answer(True)` is correct (not `answer(sorry)`).
    77→- [x] The RHS (`P`) formalizes the **question as originally asked**, so that `answer(True)` means "yes".
    78→  - `∀ k : ℕ, ∃ N : ℕ, ∀ A : Finset ℤ, A.card ≥ N → (subsetSums A ∪ subsetProducts A).card ≥ A.card ^ k`
    79→- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(True)`, not as theorem parameters.
    80→  - All quantifiers (`∀ k`, `∃ N`, `∀ A`) are on the RHS of `↔`.
    81→
    82→## 7. Category Attribute
    83→
    84→- [x] Every `theorem` has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`.
    85→- [x] The value is valid: `research solved`.
    86→  - Appropriate since Chang [Ch03] solved the problem but no formal proof is linked.
    87→- [x] Not marked `research open` (so no conflict with the sorry-free linter check).
    88→
    89→## 8. AMS Attribute
    90→
    91→- [x] Every `theorem` has **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
    92→- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed: `5 11`.
    93→- [x] Numbers are in **ascending numeric order**: 5 < 11.
    94→- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    95→- [x] Both numbers are valid AMS MSC2020 codes (5 = Combinatorics, 11 = Number theory).
    96→
    97→## 9. Proof Bodies
    98→
    99→- [x] Proof uses `by sorry` (multi-line form: `:= by\n  sorry`).
   100→  - Acceptable per checklist — multi-line proofs with `sorry` are fine when the theorem statement spans multiple lines.
   101→- [x] No `sorry` appears in `def` or `abbrev` declarations.
   102→- [x] N/A — file is not under `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
   103→- [x] No informal proof sketches included (problem is solved, not open, but no sketch is present either way).
   104→- [x] No `native_decide`.
   105→
   106→## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
   107→
   108→- N/A — no variants present. The file contains a single main theorem `erdos_53`.
   109→
   110→## 11. Code Quality
   111→
   112→- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
   113→- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable (`∀`, `∃`, `∈`, `≥`, `∑`, `∏`, `∪`, `ℕ`, `ℤ`).
   114→- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
   115→- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
   116→- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
   117→- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
   118→
   119→## 12. Build Verification
   120→
   121→- [ ] `lake build` completes with **zero errors**.
   122→  - Not verified — requires running the build.
   123→- [ ] All five custom linters pass.
   124→  - Not verified — requires running the build.
   125→
   126→---
   127→
   128→## Summary
   129→
   130→**Result: PASS** (pending build verification)
   131→
   132→The file is clean and fully conformant with all style and structural requirements. The copyright header, import, module docstring, namespace, definitions, theorem statement, `answer()` usage, category/AMS attributes, proof body, and code quality all check out. The only unchecked items are (1) optional `@[category test]` sanity checks on the helper definitions, which is a recommendation not a requirement, and (2) build verification, which requires running `lake build`.

file.content:
# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist — `deepmind/53.lean`

Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
without an explicit justification.

---

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
  - Lines 1–15 match the required block verbatim.
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line).
  - Line 17: `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
  - Lines 19–32.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 53`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source.
  - `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/53](https://www.erdosproblems.com/53)`
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
  - Describes the sum-or-product-of-distinct-elements condition and notes the problem was solved by Chang.
- [x] Optional: bibliography entries for related papers.
  - Two entries: `[ErSz83]` (Erdős–Szemerédi) and `[Ch03]` (Chang).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside a namespace (`namespace Erdos53 ... end Erdos53`).
  - Lines 36 and 67.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos53`).
- [x] `open` statements appear **before** the namespace block.
  - Line 34: `open Finset BigOperators` precedes `namespace Erdos53` on line 36.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] Helper `def` declarations have a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
  - `subsetSums` (lines 38–43) and `subsetProducts` (lines 45–50) both have docstrings.
- [x] Definitions that involve classical choice or nonconstructive operations are marked `noncomputable`.
  - N/A — both definitions are computable (`Finset.powerset.image` over decidable operations).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Types and Props use `UpperCamelCase`.
  - N/A — no custom types or Props defined.
- [x] Functions returning types use `lowerCamelCase`.
  - `subsetSums` and `subsetProducts` are correctly `lowerCamelCase`.
- [x] Use `local notation` for problem-specific notation, scoped inside the namespace.
  - N/A — no custom notation used.
- [ ] Consider adding `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for new definitions.
  - Not present. Optional but would improve confidence that `subsetSums` and `subsetProducts` behave as expected (e.g., a test that `subsetSums {1,2} = {0,1,2,3}`).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Benchmark problems use the `theorem` keyword.
  - Line 61: `theorem erdos_53`.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_53` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions: `snake_case` for terms of Props.
- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` attributes on the **same attribute line**.
  - Line 60: `@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]`.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
  - Lines 52–58.
- [x] Theorem docstring references source papers.
  - References `[ErSz83]` and `[Ch03]`.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem asks: *"Is it true that, for every k, if |A| is sufficiently large depending on k, then there are at least |A|^k many integers which are either the sum or product of distinct elements of A?"*

This is a **yes/no question** ("Is it true that..."), so **Case 1** applies.

- [x] The theorem type is `answer(True) ↔ P`.
  - The problem is solved affirmatively by Chang [Ch03], so `answer(True)` is correct (not `answer(sorry)`).
- [x] The RHS (`P`) formalizes the **question as originally asked**, so that `answer(True)` means "yes".
  - `∀ k : ℕ, ∃ N : ℕ, ∀ A : Finset ℤ, A.card ≥ N → (subsetSums A ∪ subsetProducts A).card ≥ A.card ^ k`
- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(True)`, not as theorem parameters.
  - All quantifiers (`∀ k`, `∃ N`, `∀ A`) are on the RHS of `↔`.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Every `theorem` has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`.
- [x] The value is valid: `research solved`.
  - Appropriate since Chang [Ch03] solved the problem but no formal proof is linked.
- [x] Not marked `research open` (so no conflict with the sorry-free linter check).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Every `theorem` has **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed: `5 11`.
- [x] Numbers are in **ascending numeric order**: 5 < 11.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both numbers are valid AMS MSC2020 codes (5 = Combinatorics, 11 = Number theory).

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Proof uses `by sorry` (multi-line form: `:= by\n  sorry`).
  - Acceptable per checklist — multi-line proofs with `sorry` are fine when the theorem statement spans multiple lines.
- [x] No `sorry` appears in `def` or `abbrev` declarations.
- [x] N/A — file is not under `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
- [x] No informal proof sketches included (problem is solved, not open, but no sketch is present either way).
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A — no variants present. The file contains a single main theorem `erdos_53`.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable (`∀`, `∃`, `∈`, `≥`, `∑`, `∏`, `∪`, `ℕ`, `ℤ`).
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` completes with **zero errors**.
  - Not verified — requires running the build.
- [ ] All five custom linters pass.
  - Not verified — requires running the build.

---

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (pending build verification)

The file is clean and fully conformant with all style and structural requirements. The copyright header, import, module docstring, namespace, definitions, theorem statement, `answer()` usage, category/AMS attributes, proof body, and code quality all check out. The only unchecked items are (1) optional `@[category test]` sanity checks on the helper definitions, which is a recommendation not a requirement, and (2) build verification, which requires running `lake build`.

content.text: The review passed with **Result: PASS**. No changes needed.
